categories.name: Breakfast Combos
categories.description: All-Star Special and egg breakfast platters

categories.name: Eggs & Omelets
categories.description: Scrambled eggs, cheese eggs, and Toddle House omelets

categories.name: Hash Browns
categories.description: Regular, large, and triple hash browns with toppings

categories.name: Sandwiches & Melts
categories.description: Breakfast sandwiches, Texas melts, and patty melt

categories.name: Dinner Entrees
categories.description: Chicken, steak, pork chop, and ham dinners

categories.name: Sides
categories.description: Bacon, sausage, grits, toast, salads, and more

ingredients.name: Classic Waffle
ingredients.notes: Signature pecan waffle iron. 55g carbs — the biggest calorie load at WH.

ingredients.name: 2 Eggs Scrambled
ingredients.notes: Best protein-per-calorie item on the menu. 12g protein for 180 cal.

ingredients.name: 1 Egg Scrambled
ingredients.notes: Single egg — half the All-Star serving.

ingredients.name: Cheese 'N Eggs (2 eggs with cheese)
ingredients.notes: Adds 100 cal and 5g protein vs plain eggs. Cheese adds 500mg sodium.

ingredients.name: Hash Browns (Regular)
ingredients.notes: Plain/scattered. Only 190 cal — it's the toppings that destroy it.

ingredients.name: Hash Browns (Large)
ingredients.notes: Double the regular. Used in all hashbrown bowls.

ingredients.name: Bacon
ingredients.notes: 140 cal for 8g protein. Not terrible — sausage is worse.

ingredients.name: Sausage Patty
ingredients.notes: Highest fat meat on the menu. 220 of 260 cal come from fat.

ingredients.name: City Ham
ingredients.notes: BEST protein-to-calorie meat. 15g protein, 110 cal. High sodium (740mg).

ingredients.name: Country Ham
ingredients.notes: 32g protein but 1720mg sodium. City ham is the leaner swap.

ingredients.name: Grilled Chicken Breast
ingredients.notes: Best single item on the menu for macros. 29g protein, 140 cal, 2.5g fat.

ingredients.name: Grits
ingredients.notes: 90 cal, mostly carbs. Better than hash browns if you want less fat.

ingredients.name: White Toast (2 slices)
ingredients.notes: 230 cal for toast — buttered on the grill. High fat for bread.

ingredients.name: Wheat Toast (2 slices)
ingredients.notes: Slightly better than white — 4g fiber, double the protein.

ingredients.name: Raisin Toast (2 slices)
ingredients.notes: 300 cal with 16g sugar. Worst toast option for fat loss.

ingredients.name: Texas Angus Patty Melt
ingredients.notes: 730 cal, 50g fat, only 26g protein. One of the worst macro ratios on the menu.

ingredients.name: Texas Grilled Chicken Melt
ingredients.notes: Best Texas melt option — 41g protein. Still 660 cal though.

ingredients.name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich Deluxe
ingredients.notes: 33g protein, 490 cal. Solid sandwich option if you skip fries/hash browns.

ingredients.name: Angus 1/4 LB Cheeseburger Deluxe
ingredients.notes: 620 cal for only 22g protein. 66% of calories from fat.

ingredients.name: Angus 1/4 LB Hamburger Deluxe
ingredients.notes: Skip the cheese and save 60 cal. Still a fat bomb.

ingredients.name: Chicken Dinner
ingredients.notes: Grilled chicken with hash browns and toast. 37g protein. Decent.

ingredients.name: Sirloin Steak Dinner
ingredients.notes: Sirloin steak + sides. 615 cal, 29g protein.

ingredients.name: American Cheese (2 slices)
ingredients.notes: 100 cal, 80 from fat. 5g protein not worth the 500mg sodium.

ingredients.name: Sliced Tomatoes
ingredients.notes: 10 cal. Free swap for hash browns or grits.

ingredients.name: Garden Salad (no dressing)
ingredients.notes: 35 cal side salad. Skip dressing or ask for vinegar.

template_meals.name: The All-Star Special
template_meals.description: The classic WH order: waffle, 2 eggs, hash browns, bacon, and white toast. The whole menu on one plate.

template_meals.name: Waffle + Hash Browns + Bacon
template_meals.description: The go-to simple order. Waffle, scattered hash browns, and a side of bacon.

template_meals.name: Patty Melt + Hash Browns
template_meals.description: Texas Angus Patty Melt with a side of hash browns. The late-night classic.

template_meals.name: Angus Cheeseburger + Hash Browns
template_meals.description: Quarter-pound Angus cheeseburger with hash browns on the side.

template_meals.name: Sausage Egg & Cheese Hash Brown Bowl
template_meals.description: Large hash browns loaded with sausage, scrambled eggs, and melted American cheese.

template_meals.name: Lean Swap: Egg & Ham Plate
template_meals.description: 2 scrambled eggs, city ham, sliced tomatoes. Maximum protein, minimum carbs — no waffle, no toast, no hash browns.
template_meals.swap_rationale: The All-Star is 1,150 cal with only 35g protein — that's 33 cal per gram of protein. This swap: 300 cal, 28g protein — 11 cal per gram of protein. You save 850 calories. The waffle (410 cal, 8g protein) and toast (230 cal, 4g protein) were adding 640 cal for just 12g protein. The eggs and city ham are doing all the real work.

template_meals.name: Lean Swap: Grilled Chicken & Eggs
template_meals.description: Grilled chicken breast with 2 scrambled eggs and sliced tomatoes. 42g protein for 330 cal — the best macro meal at Waffle House.
template_meals.swap_rationale: Waffle + hash browns + bacon: 740 cal, 19g protein. This swap: 330 cal, 42g protein. You get MORE THAN DOUBLE the protein for LESS THAN HALF the calories. The waffle was 410 cal with 8g protein — that's a 55:1 carb-to-protein ratio. The grilled chicken is 140 cal with 29g protein. That's the swap that matters.

template_meals.name: Lean Swap: Grilled Chicken Sandwich + Salad
template_meals.description: Grilled chicken sandwich deluxe with a garden salad instead of hash browns. Keeps the sandwich, drops the starch.
template_meals.swap_rationale: Patty melt + hash browns: 920 cal, 29g protein. This swap: 525 cal, 35g protein. You save 395 calories and gain 6g protein. The patty melt is 730 cal for 26g protein — the Texas toast and cheese are adding 300+ cal with almost no protein. The grilled chicken sandwich gives you more protein in a smaller package, and the salad replaces empty hash brown carbs with fiber.

template_meals.name: Lean Swap: Ham Egg Bowl (plain hash browns)
template_meals.description: Regular hash browns with city ham, 2 eggs, and tomatoes. Same bowl concept, leaner protein, smaller portion.
template_meals.swap_rationale: Sausage egg cheese bowl: 920 cal, 32g protein. This swap: 490 cal, 31g protein. You save 430 calories and keep 97% of the protein. The sausage (260 cal, 10g protein) swaps for city ham (110 cal, 15g protein) — more protein, less than half the calories. Dropping cheese saves another 100 cal. Regular hash browns instead of large cuts 190 cal. Same satisfaction, half the damage.

template_meals.name: Lean Swap: Chicken Dinner (skip the burger)
template_meals.description: Chicken dinner instead of a cheeseburger meal. Same restaurant, same price point, completely different macros.
template_meals.swap_rationale: Cheeseburger + hash browns: 810 cal, 25g protein. Chicken dinner: 565 cal, 37g protein. You save 245 calories and gain 12g protein. The Angus patty is 620 cal for 22g protein — the bun, cheese, and toppings are eating your calorie budget. The chicken dinner includes hash browns and toast already, so you're not losing any sides. More food, more protein, fewer calories.
